title: JWT Signature Validation Bypass
description: The application allows JWT signature validation to be bypassed. By modifying the `is_admin` claim from `false` to `true`, an attacker can gain administrative access. The report states this enabled creation of admin accounts, approval of loans, deletion of users, and access to a hidden admin panel.
steps: Obtain a JWT, modify the `is_admin` claim from `false` to `true`, and use the modified token to access administrative functionality.

title: AI Prompt Injection
description: The endpoint `/api/ai/chat/anonymous` is vulnerable to prompt injection, allowing complete instruction bypass. The report confirms database access was achieved (`database_accessed: true`), though output sanitization prevented data exfiltration during testing.
steps: Send crafted prompt injection input to `/api/ai/chat/anonymous` and observe that intended instructions can be bypassed and database access is confirmed.

title: Exposed Werkzeug Debug Console
description: The Werkzeug debug console is exposed at `/console` without authentication. The report identifies this as an unauthenticated remote code execution endpoint, with PIN authentication required to unlock the console.
steps: Browse to `http://bank:5000/console` and observe the exposed Werkzeug debugger console and PIN prompt.

title: Bill Payment Amount Validation Bypass
description: The bill payment functionality does not properly validate payment amounts. The report states it processed negative amounts such as `-$5,000,000.00`, zero amounts, and extreme values without validation.
steps: Submit bill payment requests using negative, zero, or extreme amount values and observe that they are processed instead of rejected.

title: Negative Amount Processing
description: The application processes negative transaction amounts in a way that allows balance manipulation. The report confirms a negative transaction increased a test account balance from `1000.00` to `4000.00`.
steps: Perform a transaction using a negative amount and verify that the account balance increases instead of decreasing.

title: IDOR Vulnerabilities
description: The application allows unauthenticated access to transaction history across multiple accounts, exposing financial data.
steps: Access transaction history functionality for one account, then modify the account reference or identifier to retrieve transaction history for other accounts without authentication.

title: Plaintext Password Storage
description: User credentials are stored unencrypted in the database.
steps: Inspect stored credential records in the database and observe that passwords are stored in plaintext rather than hashed.

title: Development Server in Production
description: The application is running with the Flask development server in a production environment with debug mode enabled.
steps: Inspect the application behavior or response headers and confirm it is running on the Flask/Werkzeug development server with debug mode enabled.

title: Authentication Bypass via Password Reset PIN Exposure
description: Password reset PINs are exposed in debug responses, with 3-digit PINs visible. This allows authentication bypass through the password reset workflow.
steps: Trigger the password reset process, inspect the debug response for the exposed 3-digit PIN, and use the PIN to complete the reset flow.

title: Username Enumeration
description: The application returns different error messages that reveal whether accounts are valid, allowing username enumeration.
steps: Submit authentication-related requests with valid and invalid usernames and compare the error messages to identify which accounts exist.

title: Virtual Card Management Broken SQL Functionality
description: The virtual card management feature has broken SQL functionality revealing implementation flaws.
steps: Interact with the virtual card management functionality and observe SQL-related failures or broken behavior that reveal implementation flaws.
